title: Chief Ikari changed after that day
display_title: Chief Ikari changed after that day
wikitext:
[]: "Chief Ikari changed after that day"             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:54 am "Ritchan, Chief Ikari changed after that day." These words are spoken by Naoko Akagi during episode #21', referring to a change in Gendou after Yui's death. This line has puzzled me for quite some time as I couldn't spot what this obvious change was. We know that Gendou became despondent and withdrawn upon the death of Yui, and this is indeed what Naoko is referring to. But her words always seemed a little cerebral to me. In fact, this stark change in Gendou is represented visually and rather obviously in the show itself. If like me, you are rather slow on the emotional uptake, this post may be useful in understanding Gendou's character. For everyone else, it might make a useful link. (This post uses the new Wiki image system, so you can click on most of the images below to go to their corresponding wiki pages to get larger versions) Before Yui's death, Gendou is almost always shown sporting a sly grin; smirking as though the world is his own private joke. This is particularly striking when contrasted with his later demeanour. [wkimg width=200]21 gendo grin 1.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]21 gendo grin 2.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]21 gendo grin 3.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]21 gendo grin 4.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]21 gendo grin 5.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]21_gendo_fuyutsuki_meets_naoko.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]21 gendo grin 7.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]21 gendo grin 8.jpg[/wkimg] For narrative reasons, this grin is usually directed at Fuyutsuki. However, there are instances of the younger Gendou simply grinning. With Keel during the UN report, and in his photograph with Naoko and Ritsuko, [wkimg width=200]21 gendo grin 6.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]23_gendo_photo_closeup.jpg[/wkimg] and with Fuyutsuki and Yui in their group photograph [wkimg width=300]Hiramatsu_yui,gendo,kozo.jpg[/wkimg] The young Gendou is not even above cracking a sarcastic joke—something his later self never resorts to. Episode #21' wrote: FUYUTSUKI :I did a thorough investigation of your assets. I'm sure raising a child is expensive, but isn't the amount rather large for you to have privately? IKARI :You impress me, Professor Fuyutsuki. Why not transfer to the department of economics?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jan 09, 2010 1:02 am Nice work OMF. Honestly I think this is something that most of us intuitively understand but it's nice to see such a detailed visual presentation. I can't really think of any other visual clues, but a narrative clue is that we know Fuyu meets Gendo after Gendo got into a physical fight which stressed a somewhat outgoing personality. After Yui's death though there's never any indication that he has any social life or any life at all outside of Nerv. It really doesn't help that Anno constantly (consciously) keeps us at a distance from Gendo. It's all about how everyone sees him from a distance since he won't let anyone in.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 09, 2010 1:22 am Wow, great job OMF! I had never really noticed that before. As for his beard and glasses. I believe the beard is more of a age indicator. His younger self is portrayed without a beard to show just how much younger he really is, and possibly to exemplify his parallel with Shinji. With his glasses, I think it might be a way to shade his eyes from the outside world. The eyes being the "window to the soul" and all goes with how he withdraws himself after Yui's death, hence the shading over his eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 09, 2010 1:26 am I'd suggest the beard and tinted glasses, as well as his gloves, are physical tokens of the symbolic walls he puts up to keep others at bay. He effectively masks himself and cuts off the world from his senses at the same time. These details are essentially to Gendo what the S-Dat is to Shinji, only more so.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 09, 2010 2:05 am Just more fuel to the "Gendo is Adult Shinji" fire.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Jan 09, 2010 7:02 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 09, 2010 7:07 am Well, his first pair (the non-tinted ones) were destroyed when he dropped them while opening the entry plug to Rei's failed activation test with Eva-00, and partly melted. Why would he decide to wear tinted ones after that point?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Jan 09, 2010 7:11 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Jan 09, 2010 7:44 am Ornette wrote: Well, his first pair (the non-tinted ones) were destroyed when he dropped them while opening the entry plug to Rei's failed activation test with Eva-00, and partly melted. Why would he decide to wear tinted ones after that point?

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sat Jan 09, 2010 7:48 am Ornette wrote: Well, his first pair (the non-tinted ones) were destroyed when he dropped them while opening the entry plug to Rei's failed activation test with Eva-00, and partly melted. Why would he decide to wear tinted ones after that point?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Jan 09, 2010 8:19 am People with glasses push them up all the time, so that's hardly a Gendo trait. Doesn't he push his old glasses up in Episode 05, during the Eva-00 activation experiment? OMF, take note of the fact that he's rather unsmiling during the Eva-01 contact experiment, and Yui was the one who brought Shinji. Gendo probably decided to humor her request because he had no reason to refuse. I suspect it was the stress of work during that time that made the loss of Yui even harder to bear. Gendo's glasses don't seem to have very high power, so I guess he developed low-grade myopia. Some people get it late. The shift to tinted glasses, however, seem an attempt to hide his eyes and make it harder to guess his true emotional state. A beard serves to camouflage a narrow face, so he may have grown it as he aged so his face appeared fuller, and thus, younger (and...well, the implications are obvious). The real explanation for the beard seems to be simple: it prevents audience members from noticing how much Shinji resembles him.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sat Jan 09, 2010 8:32 am UrsusArctos wrote: The real explanation for the beard seems to be simple: it prevents audience members from noticing how much Shinji resembles him.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sat Jan 09, 2010 8:40 am holy crap that post could be mistaken for a wiki page. It also made me realize what I want more than ever is a spin off show about gendo in his golden years.

[]:             Quote Post by Natsuka_Chie » Sat Jan 09, 2010 11:24 am GasmaskAvenger wrote: UrsusArctos wrote: The real explanation for the beard seems to be simple: it prevents audience members from noticing how much Shinji resembles him.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 09, 2010 11:50 am I always thought the Gendo to Gendo change was, well, sad considering how honestly happy Gendo is around Yui and Shinji (when a baby/little). Makes me wonder if Gendo was actually a loving husband/father of sorts in his early days or what (Yui's "sweet person" line aside). Also, I thought that image cut of stern Gendo just before the Yui-CE was foreshadowing to his attitude change. As for the beard and glasses, they at least make him attractive.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:24 pm Well if the alternate reality is any good indication of what he'd have been like with yui still around, he probably would have been a downright awesome dad, Seeing as Shinji is happy there.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:41 pm But he had his nose buried in that newspaper the whole time! Ironically, in the GoS2 spin-offs (game & manga), he's still the Gendo we know and love/hate. (Even more ironic: in both spin-offs of the AU world, the Eva are around and need pilots!) GoS2 volume 5 even features teenage Gendo and Yui (Kaworu too), where Gendo is an angry youth. He gets into fights because his mom abandoned him for some guy (no mention of Gendo's dad) and he's forced to live with his grandma.

[]:             Quote Post by Starasp » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:43 pm In most of the Love Eva mangas, Gendo is usually reading his newspaper, and forgetting to talk to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:44 pm "Chief Ikari's heart grew three sizes that day..."

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Jan 09, 2010 12:49 pm ^LOL, WIN!! back to topic, nice work OMF, btw
